attacks.finding: Consult questions contain observation text only; expected labels are used only after candidate generation for metrics.
Survives. Local scoring is part of the pipeline and remains a bounded explanation.
Survives partially. Generic baseline improves over no pond in some cases but lacks case-specific lineage use.
Rejected by validator-clean POND_BACKED discovery and seasoning logs.
Partially rejected by recall quality audit; ambiguous and fail-closed cases remain less specific.
Controlled by fail-closed heuristic and normalized candidate caps.
Expected labels are excluded from consult prompts and candidate generation inputs.
Initial candidates are written before pathway evolution and metrics.

invalidated_claims: Pond recall alone removes the local deterministic scoring explanation.
Generic consult pressure is fully ruled out.
surviving_claims: Required discovery and seasoning consult logs are pond-backed and validator-clean.
Specific traceable recall appeared before candidate generation for 19 held-out cases.
Pond-backed candidate generation improved at least one discovery metric over both baselines.
